title: God our Habitation
summary: “Because thou hast made the LORD, which is my refuge, even the most High, thy habitation; There shall no evil befall thee, neither shall any plague come nigh thy dwelling.” Psalm 91:9-10 Happy are we if the Lord Jesus Christ is our address! If God is our address, we have the biggest house in the universe indeed. While Solomon’s Temple was a wonder to behold, Solomon acknowledged that no building can ever dream of serving as God’s house. “But who is able to build him an house, seeing the heaven and heaven of heavens cannot contain him? who am I then, that I should build him an house, save only to burn sacrifice before him?” (2Chron 2:6). The good news is this: while nothing humans can build will ever accommodate God, God invites one and all to dwell in him. Hallelujah! “Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me” (John 15:4). Moses, who wrote Psalm 91, did not seem to have any secure, permanent dwelling place made of mud and bricks once he killed the Egyptian man and fled to Midian. For forty years he wandered in the desert with the people of God. He became a nomad – the word comes from Latin “nomas” and means a wandering shepherd. When one gives up a secure, permanent dwelling place for the sake of the people of God, then God himself becomes your address. No wonder Moses declares confidently that no evil, nor plague shall come near those who make the most high their habitation. God was Moses’ address!
